title: INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES - 211info
text: DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management, individual and family support plans, access to brokerage services for transportation, in-home supports or residential placement, and employment and vocational services. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a US citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 322-7554 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 322-7554 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to request an application or schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, American Sign Language (ASL), interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Deschutes County Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Disabilities Issues Case/Care Management * Disabilities Issues Disability Related Center Based Employment Disability Related Parenting Programs Disability Related Transportation Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Residential Placement Services for People with Disabilities Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - THE DROP MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH
